3072.history: Decommissioned stricken and transferred to Turkey on 23 May 1948;
commanders: T/Cdr. Merrill Kinsell Clementson, USN (28 Aug 1944 - late 1945)

3073.history: Decommissioned 23 May 1948. Stricken and transferred to Turkey 23 August 1948. Returned to the USN on 1 January 1974 to be broken up for scrap.
commanders: T/Cdr. Royce Lawrence Gross, USN (23 Sep 1944 - May 1945)
T/Lt.Cdr. Edward Conrad Blonts, Jr., USN (May 1945 - late 1945)

3074.history: Originally to be named Bocaccio but renamed Charr on 24 September 1942. Decommissioned 28 June 1969. Stricken 20 December 1971 Sold 17 August 1972 to be broken up for scrap.
commanders: T/Cdr. Francis Dennis Boyle, USN (23 Sep 1944 - Apr 1946)

3075.history: Decommissioned and stricken 23 May 1948. Transferred to Turkey 25 May 1948 being renamed Gur. Returned to the U.S.N. and sold for scrapping.
commanders: T/Cdr. Cassius Douglas Rhymes, Jr., USN (21 Oct 1944 - early 1946)

3076.history: Decommissioned and transferred to Turkey 23 May 1948. Stricken by the U.S.N. 25 May 1948
commanders: T/Cdr. Harry Benjamin Dodge, USN (26 Oct 1944 - 22 Aug 1945)
T/Lt.Cdr. Raymond Wellington Alexander, USN (22 Aug 1945 - 3 Apr 1946)

3077.history: Decommissioned and stricken 1 October 1970. Sunk while under tow near Cape Flattery, Washington.
commanders: T/Cdr. Arnold Frederick Schade, USN (15 Nov 1944 - early 1946)
Harvey Jacob Smith, Jr., USN (15 Jun 1950 - 5 Sep 1952 (1))

3078.history: USS Bullhead (Lt.Cdr. Edward Rowell Holt, Jr., USN) was sunk on her 3rd war patrol by Japanese aircraft off the north-east coast of Bali in position 08º20'S, 115º42'E. All 84 hands lost. The Japanese Army aircraft, belonging to the 73rd Chutai, based on Bali, made an attack at 0803 hrs, dropping 2 x 60 kg bombs which the pilot claimed to have hit, with oil and bubbles surfacing in the aftermath.
commanders: T/Cdr. Walter Thomas Griffith, USN (4 Dec 1944 - Jul 1945)
T/Lt.Cdr. Edward Rowell Holt, USN (Jul 1945 - 6 Aug 1945 (+))

3079.history: Decommissioned and transferred to Turkey 16 November 1950. Stricken by the U.S.N. 20 December 1950.
commanders: T/Cdr. Joseph Warford Williams, Jr., USN (9 Dec 1944 - early 1946)

3080.history: Decommissioned 24 October 1953. Laid up in the Pacific Reserve Fleet. Stricken 15 May 1970. Sold 28 December 1971 to be broken up for scrap.
commanders: T/Cdr. George William Lautrup, Jr., USN (30 Dec 1944 - 1946)

3081.history: Decommissioned 11 December 1946. Served as a Reserve Training Vessel in the 12th Naval District at Hunters Point Navy Shipyard from 11 December 1946 untill 30 June 1967. Stricken 12 February 1969. Sold 12 February 1969 to be broken up for scrap.
commanders: T/Cdr. John Sidney McCain, Jr., USN (30 Dec 1944 - 15 Aug 1945)
T/Lt.Cdr. Edward Everett Conrad, USN (15 Aug 1945 - )

3082.history: Decommissioned 10 February 1950. Laid up in the Pacific Reserve Fleet. Recommissioned 23 February 1957. Decommissioned 4 March 1966. Transferred to Italy 5 March 1966. Stricken by the U.S.N. 5 December 1977.
commanders: T/Lt.Cdr. Ernest Southard Friedrick, USN (26 Jan 1945 - 4 Jun 1948)

3083.history: Decommissioned and stricken 1 December 1970. Sunk as a target by USS Pogy (SSN-647) off Pearl Harbor, Hawaii on 27 April 1975.
commanders: T/Cdr. Charlton Lewis Murphy, USN (7 Feb 1945 - 28 Dec 1945 (1))
T/Cdr. Talbot Edward Harper, USN (28 Dec 1945 - 30 Jun 1948 (1))

3084.history: Decomissioned and stricken 20 December 1971. Sold in 1973 to be broken up for scrap. The pressure hull of the conning tower of USS Carp still exists, and is on display at Seawolf Park in Galveston TX.
commanders: Lt.Cdr. James Lightfoot Hunnicutt, USNR (28 Feb 1945 - late 45 ?)

3085.history: Decommissioned 10 December 1948. Recommissioned 24 October 1950. Decommissioned, stricken and transferred to Argentina 1 July 1971 being renamed Santa Fe. During the Falklands War in 1982 Santa-Fe was hit by British forces. Deemed too costly to economically repair as a war prize she was sunk in Grytvyken Sound, South Georgia.
commanders: Lt.Cdr. William Arthur Overton, USNR (19 Mar 1945 - 20 Jul 1948)

3086.history: Decommissioned 10 December 1948. Laid up in the Pacific Reserve Fleet. Recommissioned 24 October 1950. Decommissioned and stricken 31 July 1972. Transferred to Turkey 24 August 1973 being renamed Preveze (S-345). Prevese was stricken in 1987.
commanders: T/Lt.Cdr. William Robinson Smith, Jr., USN (6 Apr 1945 - 12 Jun 1948)

3087.history: Decommissioned, stricken and transferred to Argentina 1 July 1971 being renamed Santiago del Estero (S-22); Santiago del Estero was decommissioned by the Argentine Navy in January 1981.
commanders: Lt.Cdr. William Rolston Crutcher, USNR (28 Apr 1945 - )

3088.history: Decommissioned 27 August 1969. Stricken 10 October 1971. Sunk as a target off the Florida coast in 1976.
commanders: T/Lt.Cdr. Saverio Filippone, USN (25 May 1945 - )

3089.history: Decommissioned 12 June 1975. Stricken 27 June 1975. Museum Ship at Patriot's Point, Charleston, South Carolina.
commanders: T/Cdr. Sam(uel) Colby Loomis, USN (28 Jun 1945 - )

3090.history: Decommissioned, stricken and transferred to Turkey 21 November 1973 being renamed Canakkale (S-341).
commanders: T/Cdr. James Bizzell Grady, USN (8 Aug 1945 - Mar 1946)

3091.history: On 18 July 1949 USS Cochino put to sea for a cruise to Britain, and Arctic operations. Her group ran through a violent polar gale off Norway, and the joltings received by Cochino played their part in causing an electrical fire and battery explosion, followed by the generation of deadly hydrogen [chlorine] on 25 August. Defying the most unfavorable possible weather conditions, men of Cochino and USS Tusk (SS 426) fought to save the submarine for 14 hours, performing acts of skillful seamanship and high courage.A second battery explosion on 26 August made "Abandon Ship" the only possible order, and Cochino sank north of Norway in position 71.35N. 23.35E. All Cochino's men were rescued by the valiant Tusk who had lost seven of her own men in the attempt to save Cochino.
commanders: T/Cdr. William Alfred Stevenson, USN (25 Aug 1945 - )

3092.history: Decommissioned, stricken and transferred to Turkey 21 November 1973 being renamed TCG Ikinci Inonu (S-333). Stricken by the Turkish Navy in 1996.
commanders: T/Cdr. Edward Ellis Shelby, USN (9 Nov 1945 - 7 Jan 1946)

3093.history: Decommissioned and stricken 5 January 1972. Sold to Venezuela.
commanders: T/Lt.Cdr. Roger Warde Paine, Jr., USN (19 Dec 1945 - Apr 1946)

3094.history: Decommissioned and stricken 24 September 1969. Sold 26 June 1972 to be broken up for scrap.
commanders: T/Cdr. Paul Edward Summers, USN (5 Feb 1946 - mid 1947 ?)

3095.history: Decommissioned and stricken 15 January 1971. Sold to be broken up for scrap 12 May 1972.
commanders: T/Lt.Cdr. James Monroe Hingson, USN (18 Mar 1946 - mid 1946)

3096.history: Decommissioned, stricken and transferred to Brazil 28 July 1972 being renamed Guanabara (S-10). Stricken by the Brazilian Navy in 1983 and scrapped.

3097.history: USS Greenfish sank U-234 by a torpedo during trials approximately 40 miles north-east off Cape Cod, on the US east coast on 20 November 1947. Decommissioned, stricken and transferred to Brazil 19 December 1973 being renamed Amazonas (S-16). Stricken by the Brazilian Navy 15 October 1992 and transformed into a museum.
commanders: T/Cdr. Ralph Marion Metcalf, USN (7 Jun 1946 - 9 Nov 1946)

3098.history: Decommissioned and stricken 1 July 1971. Sold 13 July 1972 to be broken up for scrap.
commanders: T/Cdr. Evan Tyler Shepard, USN (22 Jul 1946 - Jun 1947 ?)

3099.history: Ordered as a Balao-class submarine but the yard was not ready to start construction of this class after completion of her previous contract so she was built as a Gato-class submarine. USS Golet (Cmdr James Seerley Clark) is most likely sunk by the Japanese guardboat Miya Maru, the Japanese auxiliary submarine chaser Bunzan Maru, and naval aircraft off northern Honshu, Japan in position 41º04'N, 141º31'E. All hands (82) were lost with the ship.
commanders: T/Cdr. James Markland Clement, USN (30 Nov 1943 - 8 Mar 1944)
Lt.Cdr. Philip Harold Ross, USN (8 Mar 1944 - 3 May 1944)
T/Cdr. James Seerley Clark, USN (28 May 1944 - 14 Jun 1944 (+))

3100.history: Ordered as a Balao-class submarine but the yard was not ready to start construction of this class after completion of her previous contract so she was built as a Gato-class submarine. Decommissioned 6 December 1945. Recommissioned 1 February 1950. Decommissioned 27 March 1959. Served as reserve training ship in the 5th Naval District. Stricken 30 June 1967. Sunk as a target by USS Cubera (SS 347) off Cape Henry, Virginia.
commanders: T/Lt.Cdr. Carl Tiedeman, USN (23 Dec 1943 - 15 Jan 1945)
T/Lt.Cdr. Ralph Huntington Lockwood, USN (15 Jan 1945 - 8 May 1945)

3101.history: Ordered as a Balao-class submarine but the yard was not ready to start construction of this class after completion of her previous contract so she was built as a Gato-class submarine. Decommissioned 9 February 1946. Laid up in the Pacific Reserve Fleet. Recommissioned 6 February 1952. Decommissioned 21 August 1953. Recommissioned 15 May 1954. Decommissioned 7 August 1954 and transferred to Turkey being renamed Pervese (S-22).
commanders: T/Cdr. Enrique D`Hamel Haskins, USN (26 Jan 1944 - 2 Dec 1944)
T/Cdr. Thomas Bullard Dabney, USN (2 Dec 1944 - 8 Jun 1945)

3102.history: Ordered as a Balao-class submarine but the yard was not ready to start construction of this class after completion of her previous contract so she was built as a Gato-class submarine. Decommissioned 10 May 1946. Laid up in the Pacific Reserve Fleet. Recommissioned 6 February 1952. Decommissioned 22 May 1952. Recommissioned again on 16 July 1954. Decommissioned again 7 August 1954 and transferred to Turkey while being renamed Cerbe (S-341).
commanders: T/Cdr. John Croysdale Martin, USN (1 Mar 1944 - Feb 1945)
T/Cdr. George Hays Laird, Jr., USN (Feb 1945 - 3 Mar 1945)
T/Cdr. Frank McElhany Smith, USN (3 Mar 1945 - 30 Oct 1945)

3103.history: Decommissioned 10 May 1946. Laid up in the Pacific Reserve Fleet. Recommissioned 24 May 1953. Stricken and transferred to Greece 26 July 1972 being renamed Papanikolis (S-114). Papanikolis was decommissioned the Greek Navy on 31 December 1992.
commanders: T/Cdr. Fitzhugh McMaster, USN (18 Apr 1944 - 11 Oct 1944)
T/Cdr. Francis Albert Greenup, USN (11 Oct 1944 - 17 Jul 1945)
T/Lt.Cdr. John Lyman Haines, USN (17 Jul 1945 - Dec 1945)

3104.history: Decommissioned 21 June 1946. Laid up in the Pacific Reserve Fleet. Recommissioned 5 May 1952. Decommissioned 29 July 1952. Recommissioned 10 December 1952. Decommissioned 21 April 1953 and transferred to The Netherlands being renamed Zeeleeuw (S-803). Stricken by the Royal Netherlands Navy on 2 June 1970. Sold 24 November 1970 to be broken up for scrap.
commanders: T/Cdr. Francis Worth Scanland, Jr., USN (17 May 1944 - Nov 1945)

3105.history: Decommissioned on 21 June 1946 and laid up in the Pacific Reserve Fleet. Recommissioned 5 May 1952. Decommissioned 29 July 1952. Recommissioned 10 December 1952. Decommissioned 21 February 1953 and transferred to The Netherlands being renamed HrMs Walrus (S-802). Walrus is decomissioned by the Royal Netherlands Navy on 15 July 1971 and sold to be broken up for scrap on 12 November 1971.
commanders: T/Cdr. Richard Ward Peterson, USN (19 Jun 1944 - 31 Jan 1946)

3106.history: USS Jallao was decommissioned 30 September 1946 and laid up in the Pacific Reserve Fleet. Recommissioned on 4 December 1953 after 'Guppy IIA' conversion. Decommissioned, stricken and transferred to Spain 26 June 1974 being renamed Narciso Monturiol. Decommissioned by the Spanish Navy on 12 December 1984 and scuttled off Cartagena, Spain the following year.
commanders: T/Cdr. Joseph Bryan Icenhower, USN (2 Jul 1944 - )

3107.history: USS Kete (T/Lt.Cdr. Edward Ackerman, USN) was lost on or most likely shortly after 20 March 1945 by unknown causes while en-route to Pearl Harbor via Midway from the area off Colnett Strait. All hands lost. She was due to arrive at Midway on or around 31 March 1945 and was officially declared overdue on 16 April 1945. The most likely cause of her loss is that USS Kete may have hit a mine in a recently laid Japanese minefield (about 1000 mines) to the south of Yaku Shima. A drifting mine is also always a possibility as plenty were around in the area USS Kete was operating in / passing through. It is also always possible that USS Kete was lost in a diving accident. Unless the wreck is found we will never know for sure.
commanders: T/Cdr. Royal Lawrence Rutter, USN (31 Jul 1944 - 30 Jan 1945)
T/Lt.Cdr. Edward Ackerman, USN (1 Mar 1945 - 20 Mar 1945 (+))

3108.history: Decommissioned 4 May 1946. Laid up in the Pacific Reserve Fleet. Transferred to Spain 24 October 1959 being renamed Almirante García de los Reyes (E-1). Almirante García de los Reyes (S-31) was decommissioned by the Spanish Navy on 16 September 1974. Recommissioned 1 September 1975. Decommissioned in April 1981. Stricken by the Spanish Navy on 1 April 1982 and sold to be broken up for scrap.
commanders: T/Cdr. Thomas Henley Henry, USN (8 Sep 1944 - )

3109.history: USS Lagarto (Cmdr Frank Devere Latta) is most likely sunk by the Japanese minelayer Hatsutaka (offsite link) in the Gulf of Siam in position 07º55'N, 102º00'E. All hands lost. See also this website (offsite link).
commanders: T/Cdr. Frank Devere Latta, USN (14 Oct 1944 - 3 May 1945 (+))

3110.history: Decommissioned 3 June 1946. Laid up in the Pacific Reserve Fleet. Transferred to Argentina 21 July 1960 being renamed Santa Fe (S-11). Santa Fe was broken up in 1971 for use as spare parts.
commanders: T/Cdr. William Thackeray Nelson, USN (17 Nov 1944 - 8 May 1945)
T/Lt.Cdr. Lucien Berry McDonald, USN (8 May 1945 - 17 Dec 1945)

3111.history: Decommissioned 24 June 1946. Laid up in the Pacific Reserve Fleet. Recommissioned 16 March 1959. Decommissioned and transferred to Italy 9 January 1960 being renamed Evangelista Torricelli (S-512). Decomissioned by the Italian Navy in 1976.
commanders: T/Cdr. Ovid McMaster Butler, USN (30 Dec 1944 - 3 Apr 1946)

3112.history: Decommissioned 16 June 1946. Laid up in the Pacific Reserve Fleet. Served as a Naval Reserve Training Vessel at Swan Island Naval Reserve Center, Portland, Oregon until 30 June 1967. Stricken 30 June 1967. Sold in 1979 to be broken up for scrap.
commanders: T/Cdr. Ralph Marion Metcalf, USN (9 Feb 1945 - 23 Aug 1945)

3113.history: Decommissioned, 16 March 1946. Laid up in the Pacific Reserve Fleet. Recommissioned 1 June 1960. Decommissioned 11 August 1960 and transferred to Argentina being renamed Santiago del Estero (S-12). Stricken by the Argentine Navy in 1972 and used for spare parts.
commanders: T/Cdr. Anthony Henry Dropp, USN (29 Mar 1945 - 16 Jun 1946)

3114.history: Decommissioned 1 January 1947. Laid up in the Pacific Reserve Fleet. Transferred to Turkey 18 March 1960 being renamed Piri Reis (S-343). Stricken by the Turkish navy in 1973.
commanders: T/Cdr. Vincent Ambrose Sisler, Jr., USN (30 Apr 1945 - )

3115.history: Decommissioned 31 May 1946. Laid up in the Pacific Reserve Fleet. Recommissioned 7 August 1951. Decommissioned 13 August 1952. Recommissioned 6 March 1953. Decommissioned 13 August 1971. Stricken 15 August 1973. Sunk as a target in August 1973.
commanders: T/Cdr. David Hayward McClintock, USN (22 Jun 1945 - )

3116.history: Decommissioned 15 June 1946. Laid up in the Pacific Reserve Fleet. Transferred 20 April 1960 to Turkey being renamed Hizir Reis (S-344). Sticken by the Turkish navy in 1977 and used for spare parts.
commanders: T/Cdr. John Henderson Turner, USN (17 Aug 1945 - )

3117.history: Decommissioned 14 February 1946. Laid up in the Pacific Reserve Fleet. Recommissioned 6 April 1963. Decommissioned 7 September 1963 and transferred to Brazil being renamed Rio Grande do Sol (S-11). Decommissioned by the Brazilian Navy in 1972. Scrapped in 1975.
commanders: T/Cdr. Malcolm Everett Garrison, USN (9 Oct 1943 - 21 Jun 1945)
James Gordon Glaes, USN (21 Jun 1945 - 30 Aug 1945)

3118.history: Decommissioned 25 September 1946. Laid up in the Atlantic Reserve Fleet. Recommissioned 19 June 1953. Decommissioned and transferred to Spain 1 October 1972 being renamed Narciso Monitorial (S-33). Stricken by the Spanish Navy 30 April 1977.
commanders: T/Lt.Cdr. Albert Raborn, USN (16 Oct 1943 - 12 Jul 1944)
T/Cdr. Glynn Robert Donaho, USN (12 Jul 1944 - 6 Oct 1944)
T/Cdr. Evan Tyler Shepard, USN (6 Oct 1944 - )

3119.history: Her biggest day came on September 12, 1944, when she and two other submarines surprised an 11-ship convoy and sank seven vessels. Later, Pampanito rescued more than 73 Allied prisoners of war who had been carried aboard the enemy transports. Decommissioned 15 December 1945. Laid up in the Pacific Reserve Fleet. Served as a Naval Reserve Training Ship at the Mare Island Naval Reserve Training Center from April 1960 until 20 December 1971. Stricken 20 December 1971. USS Pampanito is on display at the San Francisco Maritime Museum and she is open to the public. For more info on this ship see this website (offsite link)
commanders: Donald Arthur Scherer, USN ( - )
T/Lt.Cdr. Charles Barzzellious Jackson, Jr., USN (6 Nov 1943 - 6 Mar 1944)
T/Lt.Cdr. Paul Edward Summers, USN (6 Mar 1944 - 7 Oct 1944)
T/Capt. Frank Wesley Fenno, Jr., USN (7 Oct 1944 - 2 Jan 1945)
T/Cdr. Paul Edward Summers, USN (2 Jan 1945 - 1 May 1945)

3120.history: Decommissioned 11 December 1946. Served as a Naval Reserve Training ship at Oakland California from March 1947 until 1969. Stricken 8 November 1969. Sold 18 June 1970 to be broken up for scrap.
commanders: T/Cdr. Lawson Paterson Ramage, USN (20 Nov 1943 - 11 Dec 1944)
T/Lt.Cdr. Woodrow Wilson McCrory, USN (11 Dec 1944 - 28 Jul 1945)

3121.history: Decommissioned 12 February 1947. Recommissioned 4 October 1952. Decommissioned 1 October 1972 and transferred to Spain being renamed Cosme Garcia (S-34). Sold by the Spanish Navy in 1983 to be broken up for scrap.
commanders: T/Lt.Cdr. Antone Renkl Gallaher, USN (3 Dec 1943 - 24 Feb 1945)
T/Lt.Cdr. Oliver Walton Bagby, Jr., USN (24 Feb 1945 - Aug 1945)

3122.history: Decommissioned 29 August 1946. Stricken 25 February 1947. Used as a target at Bikini in 1946. Raised and sunk as a target 16 October 1948 off Eniwetok.
commanders: T/Lt.Cdr. Robert Hamilton Close, USN (16 Dec 1943 - 1 Oct 1944)
T/Cdr. Allan George Schnable, USN (1 Oct 1944 - 13 Sep 1945)

3123.history: Decommissioned 6 March 1946. Laid up in the Pacific Reserve Fleet. Stricken 1 March 1967. Sold 20 January 1969 to be broken up for scrap. The restored fairwater of the USS Pintado is displayed at the Admiral Nimitz - National Museum of the Pacific War, in Fredericksburg, Texas, U.S.A.
commanders: T/Lt.Cdr. Bernard Ambrose Clarey, USN (1 Jan 1944 - 4 Apr 1945)
Montrose Graham McCormick, USN (4 Apr 1945 - 14 Apr 1945 (+))
T/Lt.Cdr. Paul Edward Glenn, USN (14 Apr 1945 - 19 May 1945)
T/Cdr. Romondt Budd, USN (19 May 1945 - 29 Aug 1945)
T/Cdr. Robert Bond Lander, USN (29 Aug 1945 - )

3124.history: Decommissioned in April 1952 . Recommissioned 5 December 1952. Decommissioned and transferred to Turkey 1 July 1971 being renamed Oruc Reis (S-337). Stricken by the Turkish Navy in 1987.
commanders: T/Cdr. Frank Clements Acker, USN (19 Feb 1944 - 30 Sep 1944)
T/Lt.Cdr. Fredric B. Clarke, USN (30 Sep 1944 - 2 Oct 1944)
T/Lt.Cdr. John Borden Hess, USN (14 Oct 1944 - 5 Jun 1948)

3125.history: Decommissioned 18 September 1948. Recommissioned 26 August 1950. Decommissioned 30 September 1968. Stricken 1 October 1968. Sunk as a target by USS Sargo (SSN-583) on 31 January 1969.
commanders: T/Cdr. Orme Campbell Robbins, USN (4 Mar 1944 - 22 Dec 1944)
T/Lt.Cdr. Walter Lowry Small, USN (22 Dec 1944 - 25 Dec 1944)
Hugh Emmett Wright, USN (25 Dec 1944 - 27 Dec 1944)
Lt.Cdr. Hugh Howard Lewis, Jr., USNR (27 Dec 1944 - 23 Aug 1945)

3126.history: Decommissioned and stricken 1 March 1963. Sunk as a target by USS Swordfish (SSN-579) on 14 August 1963. For more information on this ship see this website (offsite link)
commanders: T/Cdr. Charles Elliott Loughlin, USN (11 Mar 1944 - 16 Apr 1945)
T/Lt.Cdr. Frank Nickols Shamer, USN (16 Apr 1945 - 26 Jul 1945)

3127.history: Decommissioned 1 August 1952. Recommissioned in January 1954. Decommissioned, stricken and transferred to Turkey 30 November 1970 being renamed Murat Reis (S-336). The submarine returned to the United States in June 2004 to be put on permanent display at the North Little Rock maritime museum (offsite link)
commanders: T/Cdr. Albert Marion Bontier, USN (3 Apr 1944 - 5 Jun 1944)
T/Cdr. Roy Stanley Benson, USN (5 Jun 1944 - 21 Oct 1944)
T/Lt.Cdr. Charles Donald Brown, USN (21 Oct 1944 - 2 Sep 1945)

3128.history: Decommissioned 27 June 1968. Stricken 30 June 1968. Sunk as a target off San Diego, California by USS Medregal (SS 480) on 6 February 1969.
commanders: T/Cdr. Louis Darby McGregor, Jr., USN (12 Apr 1944 - 2 Jan 1945)
Robert Lakin Gurnee, USN (2 Jan 1945 - 1 May 1945)

3129.history: Decommissioned 1 May 1952. Recommissioned 16 January 1953. Decommissioned, stricken and transferred to Spain on 1 July 1971 being renamed Isaac Peral (S-32). Stricken by the Spanish Navy in 1982. For more information on this ship see this website (offsite link)
commanders: T/Cdr. Henry Stone Monroe, USN (22 Apr 1944 - Dec 1944)
T/Lt.Cdr. Robert Bond Lander, USN (Dec 1944 - 29 Aug 1945)
Cdr. Romondt Rudd, USN (29 Aug 1945 - Mar 1946)

3130.history: Decommissioned in February 1948. Laid up in the Pacific Reserve Fleet. Recommissioned in 1964. Decommissioned and transferred to Greece 26 February 1965 being renamed Trianina (S-86). Stricken by the Greek navy in 1980.
commanders: T/Cdr. Frederick Arthur Gunn, USN (29 Apr 1944 - 25 Aug 1945)

3131.history: Decommissioned 1 August 1970. Stricken 8 August 1970. Sunk as a target by USS Salmon (SS-572).
commanders: T/Cdr. James Douglas Fulp, Jr., USN (9 May 1944 - 29 Jun 1945)
Stephen Lobdell Johnson, USN (29 Jun 1945 - Feb 1946)

3132.history: Decommissioned and stricken 2 December 1968. Sold 18 May 1973 to be broken up for scrap.
commanders: Lt.Cdr. Rob Roy McGregor, USN (16 May 1944 - 8 Jan 1945)
T/Lt.Cdr. Richard Harwood Bowers, USN (8 Jan 1945 - Nov 1945)

3133.history: Decommissioned in February 1948. Laid up in the Reserve Fleet. Recommissioned in January 1951. Decommissioned 19 February 1954. Laid up in the Pacific Reserve Fleet. Recommissioned 17 August 1957. Decommissioned 17 February 1964. Stricken 1 April 1964. Sunk off California 24 November 1964.
commanders: T/Cdr. Ralph Emerson Styles, USN (24 May 1944 - 30 Apr 1945)
Lt.Cdr. Charles Francis McGivern, USN (30 Apr 1945 - 3 Sep 1945)

3134.history: Decommissioned 27 June 1946. Laid up in the Atlantic Reserve Fleet. Served as a Naval Reserve Training Vessel in the 1st Naval District from February 1960 until 2 December 1968. Stricken 2 December 1968. Sold 18 May 1973 to be broken up for scrap.
commanders: T/Cdr. Vernon Long Lowrance, USN (3 Jun 1944 - 26 Feb 1945)
T/Cdr. Earl Twining Hydeman, USN (26 Feb 1945 - 6 Feb 1946)

3135.history: Decommissioned 15 October 1952. Recommissioned 5 June 1953. Decommissioned, stricken and transferred to Turkey 14 December 1970 being Burak Reis (S-335). Stricken by thee Turkish Navy in 1996.
commanders: T/Cdr. Roy Craig Klinker, USN (13 Jun 1944 - 29 Jul 1945)

3136.history: Decommissioned 8 September 1947. Laid up in the Atlantic Reserve Fleet. Recommissioned 8 March 1951. Decommissioned 6 April 1970. Stricken 15 August 1973. Transferred to Peru in July 1974 being renamed Pacocha (S 48).
commanders: T/Cdr. John Howard Maurer, USN (21 Jun 1944 - 8 Sep 1947)

3137.history: Spikefish is reported to have made more than 10.000 dives during her career. Decommissioned 2 April 1963. Stricken 1 May 1963. Sunk as a target off Long Island, New York in August 1964.
commanders: T/Cdr. Nicholas John Nicholas, USN (30 Jun 1944 - 24 Mar 1945)
T/Lt.Cdr. Robert Raymond Managhan, USN (24 Mar 1945 - 24 Feb 1946)

3138.history: Decommissioned and stricken 15 November 1969. Sold 3 June 1971 to be broken up for scrap.
commanders: T/Cdr. Carter Lowe Bennett, USN (17 Jul 1944 - 2 May 1945)
Lt.Cdr. Warren C. Hull, Jr., USN (2 May 1945 - )

3139.history: Decommissioned 15 November 1969. Laid up in the Atlantic Reserve Fleet. Stricken 15 August 1973. Transferred to Peru 1 July 1974 and renamed Pabellion de Pica (S-49). Decomissioned by the Peruvian Navy in 1995.
commanders: T/Cdr. Francis Michael Gambacorta, USN (30 Jul 1944 - Apr 1945)
T/Lt.Cdr. Charles Francis Leigh, USN (Apr 1945 - )

3140.history: Decommissioned and stricken 1 October 1970. Sold 3 June 1971 to be broken up for scrap.
commanders: T/Cdr. Paul Cecil Stimson, USN (7 Aug 1944 - )

3141.history: Decommissioned and stricken 15 June 1973. Sold 15 June 1973 to be broken up for scrap. More info available at this website (offsite link).
commanders: T/Cdr. George Egbert Porter, Jr., USN (22 Aug 1944 - 30 May 1945)
T/Lt.Cdr. Charles Robert Clark, Jr., USN (30 May 1945 - Apr 1946 (1))

3142.history: Decommissioned 16 June 1967. Served as a Naval Reserve Training Vessel at Detroit, Michigan until 1 July 1970. Stricken 1 July 1970. Sold June 1971 to be broken up for scrap.
commanders: T/Cdr. Bernard Francis McMahon, USN (23 Aug 1944 - 25 Jun 1945)
T/Lt.Cdr. Edward Latimer Beach, Jr., USN (25 Jun 1945 - )

3143.history: Decommissioned in 1962. Recommissioned 7 August 1963. Decommissioned and transferred to Turkey 18 August 1972 being renamed Birinci Inonu (S-346).
commanders: T/Cdr. John Joseph Foote, USN (30 Aug 1944 - 28 Jul 1945)

3144.built_by: Mare Island Navy Yard (Vallejo, California, U.S.A.)
3144.history: Decommissioned 3 May 1946. Laid up in the Pacific Reserve Fleet. Stricken 1 April 1967. Sold in 1969 to be broken up for scrap.
commanders: T/Cdr. Gordon Waite Underwood, USN (9 Mar 1944 - 26 Feb 1945)
T/Cdr. William Joseph Germershausen, Jr., USN (26 Feb 1945 - 4 Jul 1945)

3145.built_by: Mare Island Navy Yard (Vallejo, California, U.S.A.)
3145.history: Decommissioned 27 June 1946. Laid up in the Pacific Reserve Fleet. Stricken 30 June 1967. Sunk as a target on 16 September 1969 by USS Henderson (DD 785) and USS Fechteler (DD 870).
commanders: T/Cdr. Roy Milton Davenport, USN (22 May 1944 - 30 Dec 1944)
T/Cdr. Allen Russell Faust, USN (30 Dec 1944 - 31 Jul 1945)

3146.built_by: Mare Island Navy Yard (Vallejo, California, U.S.A.)
3146.history: Decommissioned 19 June 1946. Laid up in the Pacific Reserve Fleet. Recommissioned 19 August 1961. Decommissioned and transferred to Chile 12 January 1962 being renamed Simpson (SS-21). Stricken by the Chilean navy in 1982.
commanders: T/Cdr. William Shuyler Post, Jr., USN (3 Aug 1944 - 19 May 1945)
T/Cdr. Jack Murray Seymour, USN (19 May 1945 - 29 Jul 1945)

3147.built_by: Mare Island Navy Yard (Vallejo, California, U.S.A.)
3147.history: Decommissioned 26 June 1946. Laid up in the Pacific Reserve Fleet. Recommissioned in 1960. Decommissioned and transferred to Chile 23 January 1961 being renamed Thomson (SS-22). Stricken by the Chilean navy and stripped for spare parts, her hulk was sold for scrapping.
commanders: T/Cdr. Russell Kefauver, USN (18 Oct 1944 - 3 Jun 1945)
T/Lt.Cdr. John Frederick Bauer, USN (3 Jun 1945 - 27 Jul 1945)

3148.built_by: Mare Island Navy Yard (Vallejo, California, U.S.A.)
3148.history: Decommissioned 26 June 1946. Laid up in the Pacific Reserve Fleet. Recommissioned 6 September 1951. Rammed by USS Silverstein (DE 534) 28 May 1958 of Hawaii, while conducting training operations. The ship sank from damage sustained in collision.
commanders: T/Cdr. Harley Kent Nauman, USN (1 May 1945 - Oct 1945)

3149.built_by: Mare Island Navy Yard (Vallejo, California, U.S.A.)
3149.history: Completed after the war as a Guppy (streamlined) submarine. Decommissioned and stricken 1 July 1975. Sunk as a target off Norfolk, Virginia on 19 July 1979.

3150.history: Decommissioned in March 1946. Laid up in the Atlantic Reserve Fleet. Recommissioned 1 October 1950. Decommissioned 8 May 1970. Laid up in the Atlantic Reserve Fleet. Stricken 16 August 1973. Transferred to Peru 7 September 1976.
commanders: T/Cdr. William Bernard Sieglaff, USN (6 Oct 1944 - 13 Apr 1945)
T/Cdr. Thomas Slack Baskett, USN (30 Apr 1945 - 13 Jan 1946)

3151.history: Decommissioned 6 April 1946. Laid up in the Atlantic Reserve Fleet. Recommissioned 2 October 1953. Decommissioned 1 July 1971 and transferred to Turkey being renamed Ulic Ali Reis (S-338).
commanders: T/Cdr. Ernest Percival Abrahamson, USN (13 Oct 1944 - 8 Aug 1945)

3152.history: Decommissioned 30 March 1946. Recommissioned 1 November 1948. Decommissioned 1 November 1957. Laid up in the Atlantic Reserve Fleet. Recommissioned 10 March 1962. Decommissioned and stricken 27 June 1975. Sunk as a target on 25 October 1976.
commanders: T/Cdr. Hiram Cassedy, USN (25 Oct 1944 - 12 Jul 1945)
T/Lt.Cdr. Vincent Edward Schumacher, USN (12 Jul 1945 - 12 Sep 1945)

3153.history: Decommissioned 6 July 1946. Laid up in the Atlantic Reserve Fleet. Recommissioned 26 November 1952. Decommissioned and stricken 1 October 1973. Sold 11 April 1974 to be broken up for scrap.
commanders: T/Lt.Cdr. George Levick Street, 3rd, USN (6 Nov 1944 - Jul 1947)

3154.history: Decommissioned 7 February 1946. Laid up in the Atlantic Reserve Fleet. Recommissioned 13 May 1947. Decommissioned 11 March 1963. Stricken 1 April 1963. Sold to be broken up for scrap.
commanders: T/Cdr. James Donald Livingston Grant, USN (8 Dec 1944 - Aug 45)

3155.history: USS Torsk fired the last two torpedoes of the war, sinking Japanese Coastal Defense Craft No. 13 and No. 47 on 14 August 1945. The sinkings of these last Japanese ships of the war completed the Navy's mission, begun on 7 December 1941, to sweep the seas of Japanese merchant vessels and warships. After the war, Torsk's role as a training vessel helped her establish a world record of 11,884 dives. Torsk underwent a Fleet Snorkel conversion in 1952, and later was awarded the Presidential Unit Citation for her operations during the 1960 Lebanon Crisis. She later took part in the Cuban Blockade, which earned her the Navy Commendation Medal. Decommissioned on 4 March 1968 and placed in service as a Naval Reserve Training vessel at the Washington Navy Yard. Placed out of service and simultaneously struck for the Naval Register on 15 December 1971. USS Torsk was turned over to the state of Maryland to be used as a museum in the Inner Harbor at Baltimore on 26 September 1972.
commanders: T/Cdr. Bafford Edward Lewellen, USN (16 Dec 1944 - 23 Sep 1945)
Lt.Cdr. George Woodruff Forbes, Jr., USN (1948 - 1950)

3156.history: Decommissioned in April 1952. Recommissioned 27 February 1953. Decommissioned and stricken 23 March 1973. Sold 21 March 1974 to be broken up for scrap.
commanders: T/Lt.Cdr. Richard Philip Nicholson, USN (29 Dec 1944 - )

3157.history: Decommissioned, stricken and transferred to Brazil being renamed Goiaz (S-15) on 15 October 1973.

3158.history: Decommissioned, stricken and transferred to Taiwan 18 October 1973 being renamed Hai Pao (S-792).

3193.history: Transferred to the Royal Canadian Navy upon completion. Returned to the R.N. on 2 July 1945. Sold 1 January 1948.

3194.history: Beaulieu was renamed Lantan in September 1941 while under construction. Captured on the stocks by the Japanese at the fall of Hong Kong. Completed by the Japanese as a merchant.

3195.history: Looe was renamed Lyemun in September 1941 while under construction. Captured on the stocks by the Japanese at the fall of Hong Kong. Completed by the Japanese and sunk by American aircraft on 23 December 1943.

3196.history: Portland was renamed Taitam in September 1941 while under construction. Captured on the stocks by the Japanese at the fall of Hong Kong. Launched by the Japanese on 20 February 1943. Completed by the Japanese in 1944 and named W 101. Sunk by American aircraft off Cape Padaran on 12 January 1945.

3197.history: Seaford was renamed Waglan in September 1941 while under construction. Captured on the stocks by the Japanese at the fall of Hong Kong. Launched by the Japanese on 20 March 1943. Commissioned by the Japanse in 1944 as W 102. Heavily damaged by American aircraft off Keelung on 3 February 1945. Returned to the Royal Navy in 1947. Scrapped in 1948.

3198.built_by: Garden Reach Shipbuilding & Engineers Ltd. (Ranchi, India)
commanders: T/Lt. Walter Leslie Deeble, DSC, RINR (7 Aug 1943 - 19 Aug 1944)
T/Lt. John Vendrell, RINR (19 Aug 1944 - )

3199.built_by: Garden Reach Shipbuilding & Engineers Ltd. (Ranchi, India)
3199.history: Sold into merchantile service in 1948 being renamed Kennery.
